get.description: List all API keys for the current user.

schema.title: Response List Api Keys Auth Api Keys Get

post.description: Create a new API key for the current user.

get.description: Get the current balance for the user, authenticated via API Key (x-api-key) OR valid JWT (Bearer).

post.description:
Set the specific model for the authenticated API key.
Stores the Model ID in the selected_model column.

delete.description: Delete a specific API key for the current user.

post.description:
Email changes are now handled directly by the frontend via the Supabase JS SDK,
which triggers double-confirmation emails to both old and new addresses.

Send a password reset email to the authenticated user.
The user completes the reset via the link in the email.

delete.description:
Permanently delete the authenticated user's account and all associated data.

Deletion order matters — FK constraints require child rows to be removed
before parent rows. Errors are logged (not swallowed) so we know what
blocked the auth user deletion if it fails.

post.description:
Send a password reset email (unauthenticated).
Always returns the same message to prevent email enumeration.

get.description:
Validate an invite token without consuming it.
Returns the token's status so the frontend can show the right UI.

post.description:
Regenerate and resend a waitlist invite link for an approved email.

Only valid when:
- The email exists in the waitlist and is approved.
- No account has been created with this email yet (invite_used_at IS NULL).

Always returns a neutral message for emails that are not found or not yet approved
to avoid leaking waitlist state. Returns 409 when an account already exists.

Create an account using a single-use invite token from a waitlist approval email.
The token is tied to a specific email and valid for 7 days.

Uses an atomic UPDATE claim to prevent concurrent requests from consuming the same
token, with a compensating rollback if Supabase account creation subsequently fails.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's notification preferences.

schema.title: Response Get Notification Preferences Auth Notification Preferences Get

put.description:
Updates a single notification preference category for the authenticated user.
Rejects updates to 'account_security' (always enabled).

schema.title: Response List Organizations Auth Organizations Get

get.description: Get organization details along with all members and their roles.

schema.title: Response List Pending Invitations Auth Pending Invitations Get

post.description: Refresh the user session using a valid refresh token.

get.description: Fetch all steering vectors owned by the current user.

schema.title: Response List Steering Vectors Auth Steering Vectors Get

post.description: Delete multiple steering vectors owned by the current user.

delete.description: Delete a steering vector owned by the current user.

patch.description:
Update mutable fields on a steering vector (currently: label).
Only the owning user may update their own vectors.

get.description: Check if an API key is valid without checking balance or configuration.

Get the current authenticated user's information.
Enforces waitlist approval -- unapproved users get 403.
Ensures first-time OAuth users have an API key and signup credits.

post.description:
Recompute SAE baselines for user behaviors against a new (fine-tuned) model.

This endpoint loads the saved generation prompts for each behavior and
replays them against the specified model to produce fresh SAE baselines.
The new baselines are stored in the sae_behavior_baselines table, keyed
by (behavior_id, model_id), so that inference can look up the correct
baseline for whatever model the user has selected.

Request body:
    model_id: The hosted_models ID of the target model.
    behavior_ids: Optional list of behavior IDs to recompute (default: all).

Returns:
    Summary counts and per-behavior results.

get.description: Pre-flight check: does an org-level HuggingFace model repo already exist?

post.description:
Estimate training costs before starting a job.

Returns a breakdown of estimated teacher LLM token costs and GPU
compute costs based on the provided configuration.

Generate a structured tool schema from a natural language description.

Uses Google Gemini to parse the description into a tool name, typed
parameters, and usage examples. The result is returned for user review
before being added to the training configuration.

get.description:
Pre-flight check: does a HuggingFace model repo already exist?

Resolves the user's active HF token, calls HfApi.whoami() to get their
username and token scopes, then checks whether {username}/{repo_name} exists.

parameters.description: Repository name to check (without username prefix)
parameters.name: repo_name

schema.description: Repository name to check (without username prefix)
schema.title: Repo Name

delete.description: Delete the user's active HuggingFace token.

post.description: Save or update the user's HuggingFace token. Validates with HF first.

get.description: Check if the user has a HuggingFace token saved.

Check the status of a training job.
Args:
    job_id: The job execution name / training_run in the database.

List the current user's training runs.
By default returns only active (non-terminal) runs: pending, running.
Use include_terminal=true to also get completed/failed runs.
Use status_filter to get a specific status only.

schema.title: Response List Training Runs Finetuning Runs Get

patch.description:
Update mutable fields on a training run (description, icon_config).
Only the owning user may update their own runs.

post.description:
Cancel a running training job. Only the owning user may cancel.
Sends cancel request to Cloud Run and marks the run as failed.

Dismiss a training run so it no longer appears in the user's run list.
Only the owning user may dismiss their own runs.

get.description: List all saved teacher API keys for the current user (keys are masked).

schema.title: Response List Teacher Keys Finetuning Teacher Keys Get

post.description: Save a new teacher API key. Deactivates any existing active key for the same provider.

get.description: Check if the user has saved teacher API keys. Optionally filter by provider.

schema.title: Response Teacher Key Status Finetuning Teacher Keys Status Get

delete.description: Delete a saved teacher API key.

post.description: Submit a Spectra training job to Cloud Run.

get.description: List the current user's trained models.

schema.title: Response List User Models Finetuning User Models Get

get.description: Get the current user's model count and limit.

post.description:
Expire org-stored models past their migration deadline.

Intended to be called by Cloud Scheduler on a daily cadence.
Authenticated via GCP OIDC token.

For each expired model:
1. Mark user_models status = 'expired'
2. Deactivate the hosted_models row (is_active = false)
3. Delete the org HF repo (best-effort, reclaims storage)
4. Send notification email to the user

schema.title: Response Migrate All User Models Finetuning User Models Migrate All Post

delete.description: Delete a user's trained model, removing the HF repo and DB record.

post.description:
Webhook for Pub/Sub to notify when training is completed.
Associates the model with the user in hosted_models.
Authenticated via GCP OIDC token.

get.description:
List active hosted models.

Returns:
- Models that do not require authentication for everyone.
- If authenticated, also returns models accessible to the user (via their HF tokens).

schema.title: Response List Active Models Models Get

get.description: List recent invoices from Stripe for the current user.

get.description:

Looks up the Stripe customer by email and returns subscription details.

Fetch all active products and their prices from Stripe.

Returns usage metrics for the current user.
Aggregates from existing api_logs + credit_grants + subscription_entitlements tables.

Return pre-approved access rules for client-side gating.

Returns lists of allowed GitHub org logins and email domains from the
waitlist_pre_approved table.  The frontend uses these to verify GitHub
org membership (which requires a provider token only available
client-side) and for offline email-domain fallback checks.

post.description:
Submit a waitlist application.

Returns 409 if this email is already registered.
Returns 422 for invalid email format.
Pre-approved emails (by domain or explicit entry) are automatically approved.

get.description:
Check whether an email is approved for platform access.

Returns {"approved": bool, "reason": str} where reason is one of:
'pre_approved', 'approved', 'not_approved', 'not_registered', 'db_error'.
